description: Fix resource leaks and missing defensive guards in MCP server browser tools identified during backend code audit. Covers: fire-and-forget exec file deletion in analyze-site, stale activeNetworkCaptures tracking in both analyze-site and disable-network-capture, and unsafe dispatch result casting in export-har.

userStories.title: Await deleteExecFile in analyze-site executeInTab helper
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want temporary exec files created during site analysis to be reliably cleaned up so that orphaned files don't accumulate in the extension adapters directory.
acceptanceCriteria: In the executeInTab helper in analyze-site/index.ts, deleteExecFile is awaited (not fire-and-forget with void)
The await is in the finally block so deletion happens even on dispatch error
If deleteExecFile throws, the error is caught and logged (does not crash the analysis)

userStories.title: Fix stale activeNetworkCaptures entry when disable-network-capture dispatch fails
userStories.description: As a user, I want the server's network capture tracking state to be accurate even when the disable dispatch fails so that browser_export_har doesn't incorrectly think capture is still active.
acceptanceCriteria: In disable-network-capture.ts, state.activeNetworkCaptures.delete(tabId) is called regardless of whether dispatchToExtension succeeds or throws
The delete is in a finally block so it runs on both success and error paths
The dispatch result is still returned on success

userStories.title: Update activeNetworkCaptures in analyze-site network capture lifecycle
userStories.description: As a platform operator, I want the analyze-site orchestrator to maintain accurate network capture tracking state so that concurrent browser_export_har calls don't get confused by analyzer-initiated captures.
acceptanceCriteria: After the analyzer calls browser.enableNetworkCapture via dispatchToExtension, state.activeNetworkCaptures.add(tabId) is called
In the finally cleanup block, state.activeNetworkCaptures.delete(tabId) is called after disabling capture
Also in the error catch block (line ~960), state.activeNetworkCaptures.delete(tabId) is called after the best-effort disable

userStories.title: Add defensive dispatch result handling in export-har
userStories.description: As a user, I want browser_export_har to handle unexpected dispatch result shapes gracefully instead of crashing with 'Cannot read property map of undefined'.
acceptanceCriteria: requestsResult is defensively checked: if the dispatch result is an array, use it directly; if it's an object with a requests property, use that; otherwise default to an empty array
framesResult is defensively checked the same way: array, .frames property, or empty array fallback
The defensive handling matches the pattern already used in analyze-site/index.ts lines 950-956
